trivia: Lockheed's only widebody was an engineering masterpiece that arrived slightly too late and nearly took the company with it. Exactly one remains airworthy: Stargazer, which launches rockets.
Stargazer air-launches Pegasus rockets from 40,000 feet, making the last TriStar a working spaceport with wings.
funFacts: Its autoland system was decades ahead of its time.
The sole survivor launches satellites for Northrop Grumman.
